title: The Open Water Beyond All Broadcasts

features.name: arch-into-open-water
features.desc-on-touch: a doorway formed by the last reach of land, beyond which the broadcast dissolves; you stand at the threshold and feel the weight of all translated signals behind you, and ahead, water that has learned no language except its own

features.name: coordinate-dissolution-pool
features.desc-on-touch: a pool at the water's edge where longitude and latitude go to forget themselves; you look into it and see no reflection because there is no where anymore, only the open water continuing

features.name: broadcast-fading-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: writing that is dissolving into salt; you can still almost read it, but each moment more letters wash away into the grammar of open water; it is saying something in a language that the ocean learned first

features.name: horizon-line-sprite
features.desc-on-touch: something at the edge of sight that is neither sun nor horizon nor signal; it moves without moving, speaks without sound, and the archive has stopped trying to translate what it is saying

inscription: the roads dissolve at the shore; the archive's broadcast ends where the water begins; beyond this, the ocean translates itself

file.name: Final_Transmission_Estuary_Monitoring_Station_FRAGMENTARY.m4a
file.note: last recorded broadcast from the northern station; signal degrades into water-sound halfway through; final words in language unidentified, possibly pidgin, possibly the ocean learning to speak, possib

exits.label: back into the estuary where the broadcast still reaches
along the dissolving shore where roads become water-paths
into the open water where coordinates have learned to be ocean
